page: Amazon Web Services x2gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 physical cores on an arm64 architecture using the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 model running at 2.5 GHz. The system provides 32 dedicated vCPUs with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, backed by 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, translating to 16.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate solid multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48,566.12 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,488.0, both falling within the average range. Single-core performance is comparatively weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 942.0 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.31 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is robust, delivering 1894.65 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 139.81 GB/sec for large-block reads. Database operations perform well, with Passmark database operations scoring 20,286.9 and Redis SET operations achieving 36.44 million operations per second. However, LLM inference speeds are limited, with text generation on a 7B model averaging 15.54 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the x2gd.8xlarge offers high memory capacity per core, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-bound workloads that do not require x86 instruction sets. The arm64 architecture lacks support for AVX, AVX-512, and AMX extensions, which limits its suitability for heavy vector mathematics or advanced machine learning acceleration. The inclusion of 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local scratch space without requiring external block storage attachments, though the data on these instance store volumes is ephemeral.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases like Redis, relational database management systems, caching layers, and large-scale data analytics. It is also suitable for multi-threaded software compilation and static web serving, but is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or heavy AI inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors running at 2.5 GHz. It delivers 512.0 GB of system memory (16.0 GB per core) and includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage alongside a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48,566.12 and 36.44 million Redis SET operations per second, though single-core performance remains weak. Lacking x86-specific vector extensions like AVX-512, this instance trades raw single-threaded compute for high memory density and local storage throughput. It is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 32 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD storage, designed for in-memory databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 32 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe storage.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring high RAM density and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores on arm64 architecture
512.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Strong database performance with 36.44 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
